```
Inline responses... By the way, the original thread was talking about
parsing using unstring, which somehow expanded into the buzzwords. I
was talking about the parsing and business rules processing.

jce wrote:
> 
> "gary drummond" <gdrumm@sbcglobal.net> wrote in message
…[36 more quoted lines elided]…
> The buzzwords here are what exactly? Object ? Dynamic?  Tree?

manage a system-A proper system would manage itself-1100/2200 OS.

manage simultaneous transactions-MCS and a multi-threaded COBOL
environment works fine.

object based rating-key words parsed or an if/evaluate statement.

service models-transactions

real time dynamically changing rates-if things changed in the database,
they would be processed based on the new values.

Or are you implying that when things change, objects don't have to be
developed 
and added to the system? Do they magically handle new variables/types
without 
being coded and compiled?

Don't new entries/objects types in tables/databases require a change?
Doesn't a new object type require new coding?

Dynamic is used a lot, but it doesn't mean it can program itself.

> 
> > and imply that you know the answers to all the programming problems we
> will ever see.
> In fact I said 'As business rules matured they have left behind COBOL.'

Rules are rules. Object oriented programming is just a way to
encapsulate the 
data, process, and output in a new way. It's still input, process,
output. In 
a true transaction environment, as in an object environment, it still
has to be 
developed and programmed. Most object oriented programs seem to just
move the 
steps to another module and build another monolithic program that uses
all of 
the pieces. Dynamic linking only works when you have defined the new
object(data) 
and something else to process it's results/output.

> This does not in any way refer to all programming problems we will ever see.
> Perhaps I should rephrase what I said for the literal.
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
> language for processing all business rules".
It works, and it's a lot easier than tracing through 452 different
objects
(modules) to find the one that has to be changed.
> 
> AFAICS, the implication is non-existent. The inference is all yours.
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> 

> > To quote you-"The recent thread on UNSTRING for example highlights
> > the difficulties with even simple expression parsing.  COBOL does
…[8 more quoted lines elided]…
> 

I would go with it's more difficult in other languages. Buffer overflows
are 
a great example of problems with other languages.

> > What I said to you, was to pick your language, or environment, and do it
> > faster than the language you said " does not lend itself to this very well
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> at least what I did.

> Again, let me re-iterate. This [problem you provide] isn't anything but a
> linear system   I don't believe your challenge is comparable.  It's a *very*
> linear problem - you sort the records, you do simple parsing, you create
> output.  
No, I parse the source, create records/objects, sort the objects, 
then process for output. Object definition is flagged, and on later
versions each reference is flagged as a read, write, or branch-to.
Conditional
assembly and macros are on my to-do list...

>in fact, this is an example of the type of problem that COBOL
> handles *very* well.  I am sorry that you started with C and wasted time -
> strange for someone defending COBOL so heartily, but maybe this was the
> experience taught you the value of COBOL.
It was a learning exercise which kept me away from C and later, C++.

> The metaphorical equivalent of your response is that I've stated that
> flippers are not good for running a marathon. (I neglected to mention
…[27 more quoted lines elided]…
> reasonably handle.  
Adding total charges to date, and preparing a total charge billing
hasn't 
changed that much.


When the problem domain is determined up front to be
> object based - procedural COBOL is no longer suited as the preferred
> solution.  
Object based is the same as transaction based. Do not mix batch
processing
and transaction processing domains. The baby bells have been trying for
15 years to produce a C/C++ billing system, and it still loses pennies,
and has logic errors...

I just think that the idea of "legacy services" of yesteryear is
> coming to an end.  
Do a search on the web on convergent billing, real time
> billing, or CMR and they are not built on the same principles of yore.
Of course not. But that is a design issue, not a language issue. There
is
still a lot of batch processing around, and current thoughts are to
rewrite
in the latest language VS modify the database to incorporate the new
data.
15 years ago, using a Codsyl database system, we could add the new
record types,
reorganize the database (DRU), and run the old stuff while developing
the new 
stuff in COBOL or modifying an existing program to add the new logic.
Worked
in batch and transaction processing...

> Tell me how a COBOL programmer would most efficiently solution a real time,
> event based, flexible decision based rule engine.
Real time-no problem. 

Event based-transactions, and MCS would even collect the types in
queues, 
not for batch processing, just to allow processing by a user-specified 
number of copies at the same time, sadly it was removed from the
standard 
VS adding GDI and other API events as messages. 

Asynchronous processing was implemented by Univac/Sperry and possibly by 
others, but was dropped. Too bad because it implemented multithreaded 
processing in batch and transaction processing before even the Apple/PC
was invented... (Maybe it will come back in ANSI 2074?)

I will say that anything that may have made COBOL one of "the" languages
now that SMP and multi-xxx is common has been removed from the standards
because it was BEFORE it's time!

Please define "flexible decision based rule engine". COBOL's if and
evaluate alolow variable names to be used in tests, which is done at
execution time. (Using sets in a CODSYL database, you can have the data
stored in any order for use at execution time, and with a simple
test for any members in a long-distance or data-services set, skip
even trying to add those charges. This does imply you designed it that
way,
or added the sets and ran DRU to put the set and pointers in.)

> I am trying to do this for various political and environmental (and simple
> co-existence) reasons as we speak....in COBOL.  I'm sure that if I was
…[9 more quoted lines elided]…
> defensive.
I hope I'm not. I just think that because the PC and Unix worlds are
15-20 
years behind the capabilities of the mainframe OSs and utilities, due to 
basic design problems, we shouldn't throw the baby out with the bath
water.

Check groups.google.com in this group, 
for Internet Enabled COBOL and John Merhottin for a real world example.



> I'm not spelling the end for COBOL like others, I'm just saying it's not the
> be all end all solution for all "business rules".  I think business rules
> themselves are migrating towards a different model.
> 
> John

I agree, but what started this was the unstring/string comments, and I
haven't
seen anything as easy to use, or more flexible, in any other language.
It
goes even deeper if you think about how other languages have buffer
overflows.
COBOL will space fill or truncate (by default or if specified)...

Gary
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Designing CoBOL from scratch

_(thread depth: 8)_

- **From:** "Peter E.C. Dashwood" <dashwood@enternet.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2003-09-03T23:48:40+12:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3f55d653_3@news.athenanews.com>`
- **References:** `<bifpoa$3h6$1@peabody.colorado.edu> <bifrb0$k0j$1@hyperion.microfocus.com> <X2X2b.5928$O36.348139@twister.tampabay.rr.com> <3F4E24B4.2127CAB1@sbcglobal.net> <bip7bd$mkk$1@news.btv.ibm.com> <3F525C55.68D6EDF0@sbcglobal.net> <EBB4b.8139$zK3.399555@twister.tampabay.rr.com> <3F552E45.D21092BE@sbcglobal.net>`

```
Gary, some really "confined" responses as I don't want to enter the general
argument.

<snip>
> > > You haven't done anything but spout buzz words
> > I said 'How would you manage a system that could manage simultaneous
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
>

As every system on Earth at the moment requires external management of some
kind, I guess we have no "proper" systems <G>.

The first "proper" system, according to the above definition will be the one
that knows when and why to switch itself on and off, is aware of changing
transaction rates and allocates resources accordingly, and loads its own
media without manual intervention...
(Oops! I just realised the CDC Cyber back in the 70s came pretty close to
all those things and there are IBM mainframes that can do most of it
too...<G>)

Can we settle for "An ideal system should manage itself."?

This is not intended to decry the excellent Univac Operating system(s), just
to show that, in the heat of argument, statements can get a bit "overdone".

Anyway, I believe the argument was not about System Software, it was about
Application systems.

<more snippage>

Following is the bit I wanted to comment on...

>>>>
> Or are you implying that when things change, objects don't have to be
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
>

This is a valid observation and a fair question (stated twice).

The answer is that it depends on the Class and the environment and the
particular approach that is taken.  New properties/might well need to be
added to existing Classes/Objects, but it depends on how you implement
Object Technology.

For myself, I don't change components. ("Component" here is an Object
wrapped as COM/ActiveX). I design (very carefully...) the functionality and
data of my component and that is that. It is not "maintainable". It does
exactly what it is documented to do; no more, no less.

Now, if I needed to add another Object to the "system", I would build
another component, so in this case the answer to your question is "No,
existing Objects need not be coded and compiled in order to accommodate a
new Object."

You could say: "But hang on, Pete, you had to code and compile the new
Object..." and you'd be right, but that is subtly different from taking an
existing component apart. (Think about regression testing, just for
openers....)

And I only need to do that for as long as I insist on manually coding
components. Once there are tools to generate them from interactive iterated
"specs." (and I believe this is "just around the corner") then the answer is
"No, I don't need to code and compile new properties and Objects."

This is one of the distinctions between normal OO Programming (where you
would most likely extend or override the Class to accommodate new similar
Objects), and Component Based Design where each component is considered to
be an inviolate "building block". (Well, it is until you start looking at
"smart" components that are capable of modifying themselves, but that's
another story and beyond the scope of this discussion...)

I would stress that this is just one man's solution and other people may do
it differently, but this approach is working well for me.

> Don't new entries/objects types in tables/databases require a change?

This is a really interesting one.

I've spent the last few days building a tool that will allow me to generate
the HTML for drop down comboboxes and check lists on  dynamic Web pages.

Typically, the User sees a list and ticks or selects it and something is
"returned" to the CGI code or ASP/JavaScript on the page. What is returned
is normally a "code" and a "description" and each Web component contains a
list of these codes and descriptions, associated with a text string that the
User actually sees. It is a fairly simple job to code these list controls by
hand, and include them onto a Web page using SSI (Server Side
Include....part of dynamically generating  HTML responses, depending on the
situation). But it is tedious, and what if new entries are added to the
databases these lists are derived from? The Web page controls will require
manual update. But the target user base does not know HTML.

(I could dynamically build the controls every time the pages that host them
are loaded, but this would take more time and involve added on-line access
to the Back End RDB, added CGI activity or ADO connection, and added data
transfer...all undesirable. For the most part these lists are not highly
volatile, so it makes sense for the Users who administer the system to
generate the controls as required, off line.)

Hence, a  Desktop tool that the users can utilise, to build the necessary
HTML.

By automating what is currently a manual process (checking a database and
generating HTML) it is NOT necessary to make any changes to the existing Web
code, when new entries are added to the database.  The tool reads the
existing HTML, decides WHICH control is being rebuilt, and takes it from
there...(it derives the database/tables to access, from the Web control
name). If you wanted to add completely new controls to the Web Pages, the
tool has facilities for interactively setting up the codes, descriptions,
and visible strings, the lengths of each, the type of HTML to generate
(Check Boxes or ComboList), and pointing to the database which supports the
new control.The generated HTML is stored on the server and included into the
existing pages at run time, when appropriate, using SSI.

It is therefore fair to say that new entries and even new objects on the
Back End database require no program or HTML coding, BUT, of course, this is
limited to only a set of very simple objects, and it can work because the
existing program code has been written in a generalised way (I didn't write
it, I just interface to it, but if I had written it, I would've used the
same approach...) and does not concern itself with specific codes and
descriptions (as far as the Web Processing goes...).

> Doesn't a new object type require new coding?

As I have tried to describe above, "it ain't necessarily so...". Certainly,
it does if you are using a manual, traditional, approach.

But tradition is no longer the only option. (Unless you are working on a
site with Fortress Management...then it is, and you should ignore the above
exposition... In fact, you should ignore most of my posts...You already do?
Well, that's OK, then <G>)

I have also split hairs here between "new coding" in the sense that someone
had to write it, and "new coding" where it is generated by a tool or even
existing code...(in the latter case I haven't counted it as "code" and this
is obviously arguable. My argument would be: "If I don't have to write or
maintain it, why should I worry about it? I certainly don't see it as being
the same priority as code that I DO have to write and maintain...").


> >
> > > and imply that you know the answers to all the programming problems we
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
> output.

Sure, and jet flight is just a new way of gliding. It's still take-off, fly,
land.

OOP is a lot more than you indicate. It is more fluid (you can retrieve and
set properties without doing any process at all), and it allows behaviours
that are difficult to emulate with standard code. But, most of all (at least
for me) it is the key to components, and these can do things you CANNOT do
with standard non-OO programming.


>In a true transaction environment, as in an object environment, it still
> has to be
> developed and programmed.

Not necessarily. You could implement a third party component (or components)
that meet your requirement. Or, you could use a tool to generate it.

Times are changing.

> Most object oriented programs seem to just
> move the
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
>

I don't know whose OO Programming you've been looking at....<G>

Dynamic Linking (or "Late Binding") is fundamental to flexibility in
Component Based systems. I am bewildered by your last statement above.

<snipped remaining non-OO argument>

Pete.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Designing CoBOL from scratch

_(thread depth: 9)_

- **From:** "jce" <defaultuser@hotmail.com>
- **Date:** 2003-09-04T05:47:03+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<rlA5b.4240$y_6.263192@twister.tampabay.rr.com>`
- **References:** `<bifpoa$3h6$1@peabody.colorado.edu> <bifrb0$k0j$1@hyperion.microfocus.com> <X2X2b.5928$O36.348139@twister.tampabay.rr.com> <3F4E24B4.2127CAB1@sbcglobal.net> <bip7bd$mkk$1@news.btv.ibm.com> <3F525C55.68D6EDF0@sbcglobal.net> <EBB4b.8139$zK3.399555@twister.tampabay.rr.com> <3F552E45.D21092BE@sbcglobal.net> <3f55d653_3@news.athenanews.com>`

```
Woohoo - another semantic argument.

"Peter E.C. Dashwood" <dashwood@enternet.co.nz> wrote in message
news:3f55d653_3@news.athenanews.com...

> And I only need to do that for as long as I insist on manually coding
> components. Once there are tools to generate them from interactive
iterated
> "specs."
A spec, I take it, is not:
"A system of symbols and rules used to represent instructions to a computer;
a computer program."

> I have also split hairs here between "new coding" in the sense that
someone
> had to write it, and "new coding" where it is generated by a tool or even
> existing code...(in the latter case I haven't counted it as "code" and
this
> is obviously arguable. My argument would be: "If I don't have to write or
> maintain it, why should I worry about it? I certainly don't see it as
being
> the same priority as code that I DO have to write and maintain...").
I'd love to loan you some money sometime.
I'll prepare the contract object. Don't worry about the details...just give
me your bank account, id validation...you worry about your stuff and I'll
take care of the rest.

There will one day be code litigation and a responsibility.  Source code
will become king because sooner rather than later people will refuse to use
'components' which they cannot understand.
If you don't understand it, then get a contract with liability coverage, if
you cannot get a contract and you don't understand it, don't use it.  If you
understand it, make sure you *understand* it.

Source Code or Liability Protection - it'll be one or the other.

JCE

We have a criminal jury system which is superior to any in the world; and
its efficiency is only marred by the difficulty of finding twelve men every
day who don't know anything and can't read.
- 4th of July speech 1873
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Designing CoBOL from scratch

_(thread depth: 10)_

- **From:** "Peter E.C. Dashwood" <dashwood@enternet.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2003-09-04T19:12:47+12:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3f56e6e3_1@news.athenanews.com>`
- **References:** `<bifpoa$3h6$1@peabody.colorado.edu> <bifrb0$k0j$1@hyperion.microfocus.com> <X2X2b.5928$O36.348139@twister.tampabay.rr.com> <3F4E24B4.2127CAB1@sbcglobal.net> <bip7bd$mkk$1@news.btv.ibm.com> <3F525C55.68D6EDF0@sbcglobal.net> <EBB4b.8139$zK3.399555@twister.tampabay.rr.com> <3F552E45.D21092BE@sbcglobal.net> <3f55d653_3@news.athenanews.com> <rlA5b.4240$y_6.263192@twister.tampabay.rr.com>`

```

"jce" <defaultuser@hotmail.com> wrote in message
news:rlA5b.4240$y_6.263192@twister.tampabay.rr.com...
> Woohoo - another semantic argument.
>
…[8 more quoted lines elided]…
> "A system of symbols and rules used to represent instructions to a
computer;
> a computer program."
>
Nope. In this context, it is a statement of what a computer system is
required to do. Check the meaning of "specification"...

> > I have also split hairs here between "new coding" in the sense that
> someone
> > had to write it, and "new coding" where it is generated by a tool or
even
> > existing code...(in the latter case I haven't counted it as "code" and
> this
> > is obviously arguable. My argument would be: "If I don't have to write
or
> > maintain it, why should I worry about it? I certainly don't see it as
> > being
> > the same priority as code that I DO have to write and maintain...").

> I'd love to loan you some money sometime.

Please feel free to do so any time...<G>

> I'll prepare the contract object. Don't worry about the details...just
give
> me your bank account, id validation...you worry about your stuff and I'll
> take care of the rest.
>

Why have a contract?

Just lend me the money.

(If I generate code with a tool, there are no strings attached to what I do
with the generated code...  There COULD be, but I wouldn't buy such a tool -
That's a powerful reason why I use Fujitsu COBOL; no RTS strings attached.)

> There will one day be code litigation and a responsibility.  Source code
> will become king because sooner rather than later people will refuse to
use
> 'components' which they cannot understand.

It sounds good, but the evidence is against you. Just one major component
supplier I deal with has seen component use double in the last two years
(not counting Java Beans, which can legitimately be considered components,
or in-house production of components), and production and use of components
is continuing to rise. There are more Colleges offering Component Based
Design courses this year than ever before.

As for people using components that they cannot understand, I don't know
anyone who does that. (I correspond with a number of component developers
and users). I wouldn't do it and I wouldn't expect anyone else to use
components I produced, unless they were properly documented (usually with a
Windows or HTML help file describing all Properties, Methods, and Events,
both summarised and detailed.)

> If you don't understand it, then get a contract with liability coverage,
if
> you cannot get a contract and you don't understand it, don't use it.  If
you
> understand it, make sure you *understand* it.
>

You don't need Source code in order to understand it. That's like saying you
can't drive a car unless you are a qualified mechanical engineer. (Besides,
what if the source is in a language you don't happen to know? Are you going
to learn every computer language just so you can use someone else's code
with confidence? If you are a person who finds it difficult or impossible to
use code written by other people, then you are right; components are not for
you and Source code will be King in your Universe forever. For the rest of
us we HAVE to use code written by others because we simply don't have time
to write EVERYTHING ourselves.)

All you need is an agreement (guarantee, if you like) that the component
performs as specified.

During the last 4 years I have used a number (I honestly don't know exactly
how many, and I'm not going to search my files for the sake of this
argument) of third party components and NEVER had a problem with one. Just
over 100 people are using 1 or more components I have written. To date, I
have had no complaints...

The only thing that has limited my production of components to date is the
need for a COBOL RTS to be distributed with them. (Obviously, this applies
to components written in COBOL...) This is more of an inconvenience than a
"showstopper". The advent of .NET removes this hurdle and I expect to be
producing and marketing .NET components by next year.

Finally, regarding Source code...

It will be with us for some time yet because we simply don't have tools that
can replace it, and we don't have an alternative to Von Neumann programming
(yet...). But some very interesting research is going on even as we speak.

I stand by my previous statements with regard to the decline of Source code
and in-house "computer programming".

> Source Code or Liability Protection - it'll be one or the other.
>

...or neither <G>

Pete.
```

```
> >> If you don't understand it, then get a contract with liability
```

```
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>

As usual, you are right. It IS completely irrelevant...<G>

> The Bible was kept in Latin by the priests because nobody needed to
> actually read the thing, they just needed to have the priests interpret it
> for them.
>

As soon as the Bible was available in English and the printing presses
ensured that the "general public" (it was initially only the wealthy and
educated; most people couldn't read...) could get a copy, the "Authority"
shifted from the Priests to the Translator. (It was just less obvious. King
James addressed this by getting an "official"  translation that, backed by
Royal decree, would be above suspicion.) The first Englishman to produce the
Bible in English (if memory serves) had to do so from Belgium as it was much
too dangerous to do it in England.

Given that this was originally an analogy (I think...?) to the fact that the
source code of a component may be unintelligible to the user of it, then the
Help file provided with the component serves exactly the same pupose as the
King James Version...

(Maybe I'll start formatting these files into Chapter and Verse <G>)


> I think I'll stop now before this discussion - in a manner similar to GO
> TO vs no GO TO or Sections vs Paragraphs - shifts into a 'Religious War'.
>

As a devout Atheist, it is against my Religion to indulge in Religious
arguments...

Pete.
```

```
>
>> The Bible was kept in Latin by the priests because nobody needed to
…[7 more quoted lines elided]…
>shifted from the Priests to the Translator.

Mr Dashwood, leaving aside the Anglocentricity of this view... the first 
English translation of the New Testaments was Wycliffe's in the mid-late 
14th century.  In the early-mid 16th century Tyndale did the Pentateuch 
and after that Cloverdale revised Tyndale and did the rest of the Old 
Testament.

Both Wycliffe and Tyndale were charged with various heresies.

Gutenberg's press showed up in the mid-15th century.

So... 'as soon as the Bible was available in English' seems to translate 
into 'about a millennium and a half, give-or-take a few centuries'.

[snip]

>Given that this was originally an analogy (I think...?) to the fact that the
>source code of a component may be unintelligible to the user of it, then the
>Help file provided with the component serves exactly the same pupose as the
>King James Version...

Not just 'a user of it', Mr Dashwood, but a business expected to live by 
it.

DD
```

```
world has with Linux.  It's open source so I "know" it's more secure than
AIX, SCO, Un*x.  There is a LARGE user base here saying that source code is
important.  I know you say your TCP/IP implementation is secure, but is it
really?  The largest change to software in the last 20 years is the idea of
"open source".  Components are no doubt a large portion of the open source
community efforts; however, the source for the components is still shared
and open.

JCE
```

```
referring to the phrase 'as soon as the Bible was available in English 
*and* (emphasis added) the printing press ensured it was available to the 
general public'?  I do not believe that changes my argument; both those

would be out of their jobs if their 'Authority' were to be used badly then
the results might be different than if the 'technocrats who write their
source code' were contract-workers a half-world away whose only contact
with the Business was remotely logging-on to their network, aye... the
difference might be seen as that between losing a customer and losing a
livelihood.

DD
```

```
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> | > I think I'll stop now before this discussion - in a manner similar to
GO
> | > TO vs no GO TO or Sections vs Paragraphs - shifts into a 'Religious
> War'.
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
>
I'm a lapsed Apathetic Agnostic...started caring but still didn't know <G>

After 20 years of investigation, arrived at Quantum Mechanics...

Devout Atheism suits me perfectly. When Jehovah's Witnesses come to the door
I invite 'em in and make a cup of tea. (I strongly recommend that you try
this...it is worth it just to see the look on their faces.) After 20 minutes
of polite discussion they figure that I am "stony ground" (which, indeed, I
am...) and go away and never bother me again. Same with the Mormons...

I asked a Buddhist monk in a place called Tyangboche (in the Himalayas) what
the most important single attribute of a Religion should be, expecting him
to say "Tolerance". He surprised me by saying, without hesitation:
"Compassion".

After 30 years I am starting to understand what he meant.

Pete.
```

- **From:** "Peter E.C. Dashwood" <dashwood@enternet.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2003-09-05T03:08:00+12:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3f5756a6_1@news.athenanews.com>`
- **References:** `<bifpoa$3h6$1@peabody.colorado.edu> <bifrb0$k0j$1@hyperion.microfocus.com> <X2X2b.5928$O36.348139@twister.tampabay.rr.com> <3F4E24B4.2127CAB1@sbcglobal.net> <bip7bd$mkk$1@news.btv.ibm.com> <3F525C55.68D6EDF0@sbcglobal.net> <EBB4b.8139$zK3.399555@twister.tampabay.rr.com> <3F552E45.D21092BE@sbcglobal.net> <3f55d653_3@news.athenanews.com> <040920032055410989%josephk@iinet.net.au>`

```
"Joseph Katnic" <josephk@iinet.net.au> wrote in message
news:040920032055410989%josephk@iinet.net.au...
> In article <3f55d653_3@news.athenanews.com>, Peter E.C. Dashwood
> <dashwood@enternet.co.nz> wrote:
>
> <snip>
> > OOP is a lot more than you indicate. It is more fluid (you can retrieve
and
> > set properties without doing any process at all), and it allows
behaviours
> > that are difficult to emulate with standard code. But, most of all (at
least
> > for me) it is the key to components, and these can do things you CANNOT
do
> > with standard non-OO programming.
>
> Now this is just a tad strong.

No it isn't.

Components CAN do things you CANNOT do with standard non-OO programming.

Can you embed your called  standard COBOL routines into a Web page  (Client
OR Server) and invoke them remotely from anywhere on Earth? Can you then
have the same code run on a desktop (without re-compiling or changes of any
kind)?

If you go to this link you can download a component that does all of the
above. (Along with an explanation of how it does it and sample code to embed
it into a Web Page and your COBOL desktop applications.)

http://www.aboutlegacycoding.com/archives/v5/v50101.asp

I'll look forward to seeing your API equivalent.

Pete.
```

- **From:** "Peter E.C. Dashwood" <dashwood@enternet.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2003-09-05T19:59:59+12:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3f58439e_7@news.athenanews.com>`
- **References:** `<bifpoa$3h6$1@peabody.colorado.edu> <bifrb0$k0j$1@hyperion.microfocus.com> <X2X2b.5928$O36.348139@twister.tampabay.rr.com> <3F4E24B4.2127CAB1@sbcglobal.net> <bip7bd$mkk$1@news.btv.ibm.com> <3F525C55.68D6EDF0@sbcglobal.net> <EBB4b.8139$zK3.399555@twister.tampabay.rr.com> <3F552E45.D21092BE@sbcglobal.net> <3f55d653_3@news.athenanews.com> <040920032055410989%josephk@iinet.net.au> <3f5756a6_1@news.athenanews.com> <8VQ5b.354546$o%2.161966@sccrnsc02>`

```

"Gary" <IDontThink@so.com.so> wrote in message
news:8VQ5b.354546$o%2.161966@sccrnsc02...
> "Peter E.C. Dashwood" wrote:
> > Can you embed your called  standard COBOL routines into a Web page
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
> I may have misunderstood what you mean by "embed", but a (procedural)
COBOL
> program deployed as a CGI (that uses stdin/stdout -- i.e. accept/display)
> could be invoked from anywhere and also called directly on a desktop?
>
> Gary.
>

No Gary. CGI is server side only and it is not embedded into HTML.

It CAN be invoked from anywhere, given that the CGI is available to Web
Pages running on the server, and these can be accessed from anywhere on the
Web. If your CGI was written appropriately, you could deploy it on the
desktop, but not in a GUI environment (it would need a DOS Box to run).

A component can run on Client or Server or (GUI) desktop. It can also be
seamlessly invoked by ASP, JavaScript, and Web Programming languages.
O




>
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Designing CoBOL from scratch

_(thread depth: 11)_

- **From:** Joseph Katnic <josephk@iinet.net.au>
- **Date:** 2003-09-05T20:32:54+08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<050920032032548825%josephk@iinet.net.au>`
- **References:** `<bifpoa$3h6$1@peabody.colorado.edu> <bifrb0$k0j$1@hyperion.microfocus.com> <X2X2b.5928$O36.348139@twister.tampabay.rr.com> <3F4E24B4.2127CAB1@sbcglobal.net> <bip7bd$mkk$1@news.btv.ibm.com> <3F525C55.68D6EDF0@sbcglobal.net> <EBB4b.8139$zK3.399555@twister.tampabay.rr.com> <3F552E45.D21092BE@sbcglobal.net> <3f55d653_3@news.athenanews.com> <040920032055410989%josephk@iinet.net.au> <3f5756a6_1@news.athenanews.com>`

```
Now let's not get carried away here.

I will agree that you can find an existing component to do what you say.

However, to say that procedural CAN NEVER (read CANNOT) do the same is
perversly illogical. This is because the underlying execution model of
a CPU IS PROCEDURAL!!!.

It's not that procedural cannot do this, it is instead that a component
is easier to package and use.

So I agree that components are the way to go. I just argue that it's
not the ONLY way to go.


In article <3f5756a6_1@news.athenanews.com>, Peter E.C. Dashwood
<dashwood@enternet.co.nz> wrote:

> No it isn't.
> 
…[17 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Designing CoBOL from scratch

_(thread depth: 12)_

- **From:** "Peter E.C. Dashwood" <dashwood@enternet.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2003-09-06T12:40:56+12:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3f592e0d_1@news.athenanews.com>`
- **References:** `<bifpoa$3h6$1@peabody.colorado.edu> <bifrb0$k0j$1@hyperion.microfocus.com> <X2X2b.5928$O36.348139@twister.tampabay.rr.com> <3F4E24B4.2127CAB1@sbcglobal.net> <bip7bd$mkk$1@news.btv.ibm.com> <3F525C55.68D6EDF0@sbcglobal.net> <EBB4b.8139$zK3.399555@twister.tampabay.rr.com> <3F552E45.D21092BE@sbcglobal.net> <3f55d653_3@news.athenanews.com> <040920032055410989%josephk@iinet.net.au> <3f5756a6_1@news.athenanews.com> <050920032032548825%josephk@iinet.net.au>`

```

"Joseph Katnic" <josephk@iinet.net.au> wrote in message
news:050920032032548825%josephk@iinet.net.au...
> Now let's not get carried away here.
>

I agree not to get carried away <G>.

> I will agree that you can find an existing component to do what you say.
>
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>
Yes it is.

But it is severely limited to suggest that therefore all computer systems
must be procedural.

There is a thing called synergy. It applies to computer systems just like
most systems. The whole is very often greater than the sum of the parts.

Packaging code in a certain way happens to allow it to interact more freely
than procedural code that ISN'T packaged that way. The results are
surprising in their possibilities. DNA is a lot different to soot in your
fireplace, yet without carbon atoms neither would exist.

It is "perversely illogical" to suggest that "CAN NEVER" means the same as
"CANNOT" <G>.

I didn't say that and I don't believe that. "CANNOT"  (in the sense that I
used it) implies "at this point in time". I stand by what I said. Whats's
more, I have demonstrated it, if you took the time to follow the references
provided.

> It's not that procedural cannot do this, it is instead that a component
> is easier to package and use.
>

COBOL (actually, ANY procedural code) code cannot do some of the things
components can. That was the gist of my original statement and I stand by
it, (without getting carried away).

This may change in the future, but personally, I doubt it. It is more likely
(in my opinion) that procedural code will be replaced.

> So I agree that components are the way to go. I just argue that it's
> not the ONLY way to go.
>

No argument. While I may have vehemently advocated the use of components I
have never suggested that they are the ONLY solution to all things computer.
Components are not well suited to Batch Processing, for example. OOP is a
good solution to many things currently handled by Procedural code. I see
components as a step beyond OOP, that's all.

Pete.
<snipped previous post>
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ [Still OT] Re: Designing CoBOL from scratch

_(thread depth: 8)_

- **From:** "jce" <defaultuser@hotmail.com>
- **Date:** 2003-09-04T05:14:14+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<GSz5b.4235$y_6.261749@twister.tampabay.rr.com>`
- **References:** `<bifpoa$3h6$1@peabody.colorado.edu> <bifrb0$k0j$1@hyperion.microfocus.com> <X2X2b.5928$O36.348139@twister.tampabay.rr.com> <3F4E24B4.2127CAB1@sbcglobal.net> <bip7bd$mkk$1@news.btv.ibm.com> <3F525C55.68D6EDF0@sbcglobal.net> <EBB4b.8139$zK3.399555@twister.tampabay.rr.com> <3F552E45.D21092BE@sbcglobal.net>`

```
Disclaimer:

I believe I am biased in my current view of what I am needing to do,
research in comparitive 3rd Party tools and their implementation.
I have, unfortunately, rationalized the solution in a non procedural manner
but have to somehow translate it.
I *still* believe that newer business rules are above and beyond the normal
in-do simple stuff-out.

But thanks for you comments.  To clarify - this is not an argument but me
venting my frustrations and in many ways reaching out for help without
admitting it.

Feel free to let this end the thread if you desire, just thought I would
give you some insight into what I was saying and acknowledge the time you
took to respond.

John

"gary drummond" <gdrumm@sbcglobal.net> wrote in message
news:3F552E45.D21092BE@sbcglobal.net...
> > I said 'How would you manage a system that could manage simultaneous
> > transactions using an object based rating, customers and service models
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> manage a system-A proper system would manage itself-1100/2200 OS.
Not aware of this. A quick look isn't exactly what I was talking about but
that's neither here not there.  I learn something new *every* day.

> manage simultaneous transactions-MCS and a multi-threaded COBOL
> environment works fine.
In my case, I can use COBOL called from a multithreaded application, but it
can't initiate new threads or tasks.  I'm speaking mostly of a massive batch
process that needs to be migrated to a real time process supporting existing
rules and *new* features.  These new features lend themselves much more to
an OO representation - both from an end user setup perspective and then, as
a result, from  a processing perspective.  The idea is to build a front end
system that will handle the input into a large rules platform where new
rules can be entered.  Rules would be based on a simple subset of defined
criteria - each with an execution context with "unknown" run time data
elements / records / objects.  Example later.
We used to have an implementation in CICS but the performance was
horrible...so we're looking at a pseudo-transactional batch system (unless
someone can tell me the best way to process records in this manner
differently on MVS).


> object based rating-key words parsed or an if/evaluate statement.
IF/EVALUATE is usable; however, it's very rigidly structured and would
obviously be combined with a separate key word repository.  This is
essentially what I have, but what I'm trying to resolve is the caching of
significant portions of data in an easy to use, variably structured way.
This is something I could do very simply in a number of OO implementations;
however, I am struggling with COBOL to create a balance of caching, dbms
access and readability.  I don't have the luxury of polymorphism or
overloading or dynamic memory allocation so I am resorting to strange
internal data management. (Actually it's simple but I need to exaggerate to
defend myself :-o)
Another thing I want to do is to have objects manage and monitored outside
of the main execution loop.  In the COBOL implementation I have to
sequentially send of an update task. Unless someone can share with me a
method of kicking of a monitor that can update another processes storage (I
know this is possible because someone told me about a nice RACF trick
recently for systems programmers :-) )

> real time dynamically changing rates-if things changed in the database,
> they would be processed based on the new values.
Yes, but for performance reasons I cannot accept the dbms access path for
each unordered input.  Even with optimization, re-indexing, the repeated
read of tables is not fast enough.  I therefore need an efficient
caching/retrieval method.   I need to process at least 250 recs/sec to make
this a legitimate proposal.
I'm currently working on the basis of a fixed number of items with direct
array index as I cannot think of any other way to do this.  I've implemented
this somewhere else and saw a +30% time throughput.  I'm looking into
something similar again; however, the rules are now variable in size and
different based on records attributes as well as types.
For one I may have - if it's blue and english and cold then do a if it's of
type 1 and older than 5, another is if it's blue and english and hot and the
sky is red do b, and another might be do c based on the fact that the system
time is afternoon in Greenwich or if I've done this 10 times before for this
user and he's in a different state and he's pink and he's previously used a
blue one in another country then do d, and if this is bob and he's used 150%
more than he should have of the blue type then send an alert to his email.
I don't know of  a flexible way to build this internally and the constant
DB2 lookups would kill me for performance.  I'm not saying it cannot be done
in cobol, but it seems easier in java / c++ ;-) (Though performance of COBOL
at my site still outstrips java).

> Or are you implying that when things change, objects don't have to be
developed
> and added to the system? Do they magically handle new variables/types
> without being coded and compiled?
Not exactly, but almost.  With simple rules and design, the code would
execute based on different object entities based on parameter input, and
execution contexts.  The new contexts must obviously be built/coded in each
instance, the new types also.  In essence however, the data will be new data
based on existing types.  New objects will have a common interface for the
execution context.

> Don't new entries/objects types in tables/databases require a change?
> Doesn't a new object type require new coding?
No.Yes.  New objects in tables would not require new coding if it satisfied
a set of criteria.  A new object would require new coding.

> Dynamic is used a lot, but it doesn't mean it can program itself.
No. But it does allow rules to be build in an efficient, understood manner
in memory.  I have a hard time reproducing this in COBOL.  I have seen many
examples of COBOL reading and processing linked lists for example, but never
one creating one (if you have a link I would love to see it).  Yes - I could
mix environments and call C or PL/I but ...well I don't see why not so maybe
I'm just arguing for no good reason :-)

> > > and imply that you know the answers to all the programming problems we
> > will ever see.
> > In fact I said 'As business rules matured they have left behind COBOL.'
> Rules are rules. Object oriented programming is just a way to
> encapsulate the data, process, and output in a new way. It's still input,
process,
> output.
Yes but in many respects is more flexible.  In many respects it's more
complicated.  Every program I've ever seen is input/process/output....

> a true transaction environment, as in an object environment, it still
> has to be developed and programmed. Most object oriented programs seem to
just
> move the steps to another module and build another monolithic program that
uses
> all of  the pieces. Dynamic linking only works when you have defined the
new
> object(data) and something else to process it's results/output.
A lot of OO programmers were previously procedural programmers.
Code compiling in a C++ compiler is not necessarily OO and doesn't mean I've
learnt C++ :-)

> > As I recall - this thread lead to lengthy discussion both using
unstring,
> > and byte level checking to do what I would contend should be a fairly
simple
> > exercise.  I will accept that collectively the group provided several
simple
> > solutions - but I doubt that all languages would have had such serious
> > debate on the issue.  Perhaps the issue is less of ability to do it and
more
> > the fact that COBOL language libraries are often less shared and
publicly
> > disclosed for legal reasons (or maybe it is more difficult).
> I would go with it's more difficult in other languages. Buffer overflows
> are a great example of problems with other languages.
Similarly, truncation (ok - so read the compile messages) and a fixed size
are a great example of problems with this language.

> No, I parse the source, create records/objects, sort the objects,
> then process for output. Object definition is flagged, and on later
> versions each reference is flagged as a read, write, or branch-to.
> Conditional
> assembly and macros are on my to-do list...
I think we agree to disagree on this one.  I think this is easier done in
Java.  Look at Collection.sort() method, and StringTokenizer() where it
includes returning the delimiter.
I don't have 2 hours but I think it wouldn't take long (and eclipse is free
and it would run probably most platforms)..

> Adding total charges to date, and preparing a total charge billing
> hasn't  changed that much.
I believe it has.  Aspects of it haven't...fundametally you do pxq and tax.
But the complexity of rating has increased.
Take a typical mobile phone biller and you have a number of plans.  Now add
in the fact that the plans are perhaps combined across numbers or users and
that access is predicated on real time billing statistics (you over your
limit then you're charged more).  Now take this and combine it with other
lines - regular phones, internet, taxable services, and non taxable services
with prepaid and non prepaid and shifting criteria.  I don't think this used
to go on (I only just recently reduced myself to owning a cell phone so I
could be wrong :-)....having your car stall at a major intersection without
a phone isn't much fun )

> Object based is the same as transaction based. Do not mix batch
> processing and transaction processing domains. The baby bells have been
trying for
> 15 years to produce a C/C++ billing system, and it still loses pennies,
> and has logic errors...
I guess I'm mixing them because I have to.  I want to execute a transaction
based system that is also a batch based system :-)

> > You should read the thread on Fortress Mentality and quit being so
> > defensive.
> I hope I'm not. I just think that because the PC and Unix worlds are
> 15-20 years behind the capabilities of the mainframe OSs and utilities,
due to
> basic design problems, we shouldn't throw the baby out with the bath
> water.
Your response certainly makes it clear that you are not. This is the point I
would most emphasize - I kind of regret saying this, it was neither
professional or nice (and in spite of this being usenet I would like to be
both).
In fact, I have learnt and will go do more research :-)
I'm the staunchest defender of COBOL in my office....I get sneered at
constantly by the highly paid engineers for not modernizing.  I can always
keep them quiet though by asking "when was the last time your new UN*X
client / server app went down and needed a reboot ?"

> Check groups.google.com in this group,
> for Internet Enabled COBOL and John Merhottin for a real world example.
I wasn't really referring to "internet enabling cobol" but always need
something to read...

> > I'm not spelling the end for COBOL like others, I'm just saying it's not
the
> > be all end all solution for all "business rules".  I think business
rules
> > themselves are migrating towards a different model.
> >
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> haven't seen anything as easy to use, or more flexible, in any other
language.
> It goes even deeper if you think about how other languages have buffer
> overflows.
> COBOL will space fill or truncate (by default or if specified)...
It is flexible to the n'th degree...I think I learnt that most people don't
use/know half of what it can do.



JCE
```

```
access requirements.
```

## Thread: Reg: Copy records from one file to another based on contents of a third file. — 3 this month / 8 total

_Topics: [`Language features and syntax`](topics.md#syntax)_

_Thread started [2003-08](2003-08.md#t-ab3f0136c0)._

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Reg: Copy records from one file to another based on contents of a third file.

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** "jce" <defaultuser@hotmail.com>
- **Date:** 2003-09-01T06:41:59+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<XSB4b.8142$zK3.403015@twister.tampabay.rr.com>`
- **References:** `<de8e098d.0308290530.3c4665a0@posting.google.com> <3f4fd917.505424931@news.central.cox.net> <3f4ffe9b$1_1@news.athenanews.com> <de8e098d.0308301839.6273b2e4@posting.google.com>`

```

"ssb" <mail_ssb@yahoo.com> wrote in message
news:de8e098d.0308301839.6273b2e4@posting.google.com...
> Hi Pete and Rob,
>
> This is the most efficient way of resolving the
> problem....Unfortunately, I need to avoid using any DB2 Tables... :-(
>
Because you don't have it? You can create them dynamically  - they don't
need to be predefined by DBAs.

TWO options.

Use DFSORT or FILE-AID to get distinct name from file A into TEMP1.  (there
is a FIRST instance operator)
Use DFSORT or FILE-AID to get distinct name from file B into TEMP2.
Use DFSORT or FILE-AID to get rows from  TEMP1 & TEMP2 where count > 2

If the last step doesn't have this operation (i think it does) then:
Use DFSORT or FILE-AID to get distinct name from file A into TEMP1 add a
value 1 in column x.  (there is a FIRST instance operator)
Use DFSORT or FILE-AID to get distinct name from file B into TEMP2 add a
value 1 in column x.
Use DFSORT or FILE-AID to get rows from  TEMP1 & TEMP2 adding values in
column x  into TEMP3
Use DFSORT or FILE-AID to get rows from  TEMP3 where value in column x > 1

If you go to the DFSORT site - they have many examples - the documentation
is pretty decent.

A program would probably be easier.

Use DFSORT to sort A into TEMP1.
Use DFSORT to sort B into TEMP2.

Write short program...read TEMP1 read TEMP2

PERFORM READ-TEMP1
PERFORM READ-TEMP2

PERFORM UNTIL EOF
IF TEMP1 <  TEMP2
    PERFORM READ-TEMP1
IF TEMP1 >  TEMP2
    PERFORM READ-TEMP2
IF TEMP1 =  TEMP2
    PERFORM WRITE-TEMP1-IN-BOTH
    PERFORM READ-TEMP1
END-PERFORM.

I wrote something similar once that did this one keeping those in FILE A and
not in B, in B and not in A and in Both....shouldn't take more than an hour.
I'd give you the source but I'd have to go find it on my old machine sitting
in parts in the corner.  If you have a hard time - post it here and get some
help :-)

JCE

> Best regards...
> "Peter E.C. Dashwood" <dashwood@enternet.co.nz> wrote in message
news:<3f4ffe9b$1_1@news.athenanews.com>...
> > Snap!
> >
…[19 more quoted lines elided]…
> > > You can do it easily with SQL. Load A and C into DB2 temp tables,
extract
> >  the
> > > matches (B) with a SELECT * FROM A WHERE A.Name IN (SELECT Name FROM
C);
>
```

```
mail_ssb@yahoo.com (ssb) wrote:

>This is the most efficient way of resolving the
>problem....Unfortunately, I need to avoid using any DB2 Tables... :-(

Why? EVERYone is authorized to create temp tables. They exist in memory, not on
disk, and disappear when the job ends. 

>"Peter E.C. Dashwood" <dashwood@enternet.co.nz> wrote in message
news:<3f4ffe9b$1_1@news.athenanews.com>...

>>
```

#### ↳ Re: Reg: Copy records from one file to another based on contents of a third file.

- **From:** Kyle Kittinger <kittingr@vvm.com>
- **Date:** 2003-09-16T15:53:51-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<o34fmvotvh394gs1ngemqrfhv2huvkb8qq@4ax.com>`
- **References:** `<de8e098d.0308290530.3c4665a0@posting.google.com>`

```


Check into you sort program, DFSORT will allow you to merge the two
files together. 

with running DFSORT twice this you should be able to do what you want.

1) Take file-c and make the name field match in postion in file-a
adding a fill character to be used latter to remove just the name
records.  

OUTFIL FNAMES=FLR1,OUTREC=(5,14,32,8,2Z,22,6),
    VLFILL=C'*'

2) Then merge the two file together 


MERGE
 Merge is generally used to add records from a dataset to another
dataset. Merge allows you merge up to 16 files (This was raised to a
limit of 100 in release 14 of DFSort) . The files being merged must be
the same format and have been sorted.

Examples:
MERGE FIELDS=(5,10,CH,A)
MERGE FIELDS=(10,5,ZD,D,34,4,PD,A)
MERGE FIELDS=(3,5,A,17,3,A),FORMAT=CH

May need to add a outfil statment to exlcude the records that just
contains the name only.


OUTFIL INCLUDE=(1,10,CH,EQ,C'**********'),FNAMES=GP1


On 29 Aug 2003 06:30:58 -0700, mail_ssb@yahoo.com (ssb) wrote:

>Hi,
>
…[13 more quoted lines elided]…
>Thanks...
```

```
  http://www.cobolreport.com/faqs/cobolfaq.htm

If you have comments, corrects, deletions, or additions, please send
private email to:

Toward the top of the FAQ is a pointer to the appendix listing the most
recent significant updates and changes.

This FAQ answers such questions as (and addresses topics such as):

 - Where can I get a COBOL compiler? 
 - Is there a free COBOL compiler? 
 - Where can I contact... ?
 - What is happening with the draft of the next COBOL Standard and what
is in it? 
 - What about OO COBOL? 
 - Books about COBOL
 - COBOL Tools
 - What can/should I post in the COBOL newsgroups?
 - What about USAGE? COMP? Storage for data in xyz format? etc?
 - How do I get started with COBOL? Where can I get education?
Tutorials? Etc

and much, MUCH more
```

## Thread: Confused with Two-Level Table Program — 4 msgs

### Confused with Two-Level Table Program

- **From:** mikey@mailbox.co.za (Michael)
- **Date:** 2003-09-01T03:31:55-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<5cddb7f2.0309010231.54e77e9d@posting.google.com>`

```
Hi, I was wondering if any of you could help me...I'm busy with a
crash-course in Cobol now after I failed the inital course

I'm completely confused with a two-level table program I have to
write...I don't know how to get the values out of the input file and
into the two-level table.  I know I have to do a search, but because
of the way the input file is structured I'm really confused....

I've included the program specifications, my code and the input
files...
My code is a mess..

If you could help in anway I'd really appreciate it..


Project 1
Description:
Write a program to determine whether a customer receives a discount
based on quantity ordered, and calculate the discounted unit price and
total discounted price. Create a report that prints each customer's
order, as well as the total quantity ordered and total sales for the
company. A summary must be included at the end of the report listing
the Item Series as well as the total discount for the series.
Input (Pr1Sales.dat):
01	IN-ORDER-TRANSACTION.
	05	IN-CUST-NUM		PIC 9(8).
	05	IN-QTY-ORDERED		PIC 9(3).
	05	IN-ITEM-NUMBER		PIC X(5).
	05	IN-UNIT-PRICE		PIC 9(3)V99.
Report Layout
Austin Retail Company

CUSTOMER	ITEM	QTY	UNIT	DISCOUNTED
NUMBER	NUMBER	ORDERED	PRICE	PRICE
				
XXXXXXXX	XXXXX	999	R999.99	R999.99
				

Summary

PRODUCT SERIES	TOTAL DISCOUNT
X	R9999999.99
	

 
Processing
1.	Read the file of order records.
2.	For every record read:
a.	Determine whether the customer will receive a discount. The
discounting of an item is based on the Item Series and Quantity
Ordered. The Item Series is the first character of the Item Number.
For example, Item Number 12345 is Item Series 1, and P3764 is Series
P, and so forth.
The discount is further determined by the quantity ordered. For
example, if a customer buys 0 - 10 units of Series 1, he/she will
receive 10% discount. For 11 - 50 units, he/she will receive 20%
discount.
A file is available (Pr1Disc.dat) containing the discount values in
the following format:
01	IN-DISCOUNT.
	05	DC-ITEM-SERIES		PIC X.
	05	DC-VALUES
		OCCURS 4 TIMES.
		10	NUM-ITEMS		PIC 999.
		10	DISCOUNT		PIC 99.	
This file needs to be read into a two-dimensional table at start-up,
and referred to during processing to get the discounts.
b.	Print the customer number, item number, quantity ordered, the
original unit price, and the discounted unit price.
c.	Update the summary by adding the Item Series (if it does not
already exist), and add the customer's discount to the total discount.
The summary table must be a variable length table with a maximum of 10
items.
d.	Update the Total Quantity Ordered and Total Discount for the
company.
3.	Print the company totals for Total Quantity Ordered and Total
Discount.
4.	Print the summary.

000020 PROGRAM-ID.  OP201SRT.
000030 AUTHOR.      MICHAEL.
000040 DATE-WRITTEN.  AUGUST 2003.
000050 ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.

000070 FILE-CONTROL.
000080     SELECT SALES-FILE ASSIGN TO DISK 'A:\PR1SALES.DAT'
000090            ORGANIZATION IS LINE SEQUENTIAL.

001320INITIALIZATION.

001340			 DISCOUNT-FILE
001350          OUTPUT   PRINT-FILE.
001360     ACCEPT WS-DATE FROM DATE.
001370     PERFORM WRITE-HEADING-LINES.
001380     PERFORM LOAD-DISCOUNT-TABLE
001390	   CLOSE 	 DISCOUNT-FILE. 
001400
001410
001420AD-TABLE.
001430*************
001440
001450LOAD-DISCOUNT-TABLE.
001460     PERFORM Z-READ-DISCOUNT
001470     PERFORM LOAD-DISCOUNTS VARYING DISC-INDEX 
001480             FROM 1 BY 1 UNTIL DISC-INDEX > 8
001490             AFTER VALUE-INDEX FROM 1 BY 1 UNTIL VALUE-INDEX > 4
001500      	   OR END-DISCOUNT
001510     IF NOT END-DISCOUNT
001520        DISPLAY 'DISCOUNT TABLE TOO SMALL -RECOMPILE'
001530        DISPLAY 'RUN TERMINATING'
001540        STOP RUN.
001550        
001560 LOAD-DISCOUNTS.
001570     MOVE DC-VALUES TO DISC-VALUES(DISC-INDEX VALUE INDEX)
001580     PERFORM Z-READ-DISCOUNT.
001590

001680 SEARCH-DISOUNT.

001710SET VALUE-INDEX TO 1

001730       AT END

001750       WHEN DC-ITEM-SERIES = IN-SERIES
001760		PERFORM GET-DISCOUNT
001770		PERFORM CALCULATE-DISCOUNT      
001780		MOVE IN-CUST-NUM TO DET-CUST-NUM
001790 		MOVE IN-ITEM-NUMBER TO DET-ITEM-NUMBER
001800 		MOVE IN-QTY-ORDERED TO DET-QTY-ORDERED

002760

002780**************
002790     READ DISCOUNT-FILE
002800       AT END

002820     
002830                       
002840 PRINT-HEADINGS.
002850*****************
002860     WRITE PRINT-LINE FROM HDG-LINE-ONE AFTER 1.
002870     WRITE PRINT-LINE FROM HDG-LINE-TWO AFTER 2.
002880     WRITE PRINT-LINE FROM HDG-LINE-THREE.
002890     WRITE PRINT-LINE FROM HDG-LINE-FOUR.
002900     WRITE PRINT-LINE FROM HDG-LINE-FIVE.
002910     WRITE PRINT-LINE FROM SPACES.
002920
```

```
On 1 Sep 2003 03:31:55 -0700 mikey@mailbox.co.za (Michael) wrote:

:>Hi, I was wondering if any of you could help me...I'm busy with a
:>crash-course in Cobol now after I failed the inital course

:>I'm completely confused with a two-level table program I have to
:>write...I don't know how to get the values out of the input file and
:>into the two-level table.  I know I have to do a search, but because
:>of the way the input file is structured I'm really confused....

:>I've included the program specifications, my code and the input
:>files...
:>My code is a mess..

One would have greater belief of your effort if the code you supplied
compiled. That you should be able to do with little help, or have specific
questions about the compiler errors received.

Your load table will not work properly.

Should you manage to get a clean compile, it would be advisable to display the
table to confirm that the data was loaded properly.
```

```
001590

001621 	   MOVE IN-ITEM-NUMBER TO DET-ITEM-NUMBER.
001622     MOVE IN-QTY-ORDERED TO DET-QTY-ORDERED.
001623 	   MOVE IN-UNIT-PRICE TO DET-UNIT-PRICE.

001690*****************
001700     SET DISC-INDEX TO 1.
001710     SEARCH DISC-DETAILS

001751          SET VALUE-INDEX TO 1

001753		  AT END

001756               COMPUTE WS-DISCOUNT-AMT = 
001757                        IN-QTY-ORDERED * IN-UNIT-PRICE *
WS-DISCOUNT / 100
001758               COMPUTE WS-DISCOUNTED-AMT = 
001759			      IN-QTY-ORDERED * IN-UNIT-PRICE * (100 - WS-DISCOUNT) /
100
001769          END-SEARCH
001779     END-SEARCH.
001780		
001830

002751**************

002753       AT END
```

```
Hi Mike,

Hang in there. You're almost home free. It looks to me like you've got
the summary tbl population ok. The only thing left is to print it.
What's the problem? Do you know where/when to write it? You should
know how many entries are in the table. And you should know who they
are and how much was accumed for each, right?

If not, set me straight. What don't you know? Maybe we can figure it
out.

Regards, Jack.
```

## Thread: Indent Blues - the woe of auto. conversions to COBOL from 4th gen. lang. — 15 this month / 24 total

_Thread started [2003-08](2003-08.md#t-4027e41b86)._

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Indent Blues - the woe of auto. conversions to COBOL from 4th gen. lang.

- **From:** noname21@iname.com (J.B. Martin)
- **Date:** 2003-09-01T08:24:31-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<fed16812.0309010724.7cf292cb@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<fed16812.0308290148.50d4a43a@posting.google.com> <a3eukvg38dd9m8f2uht1b6s9ra7pp7294n@4ax.com>`

```
> Why don't you guys write your own program. Just for that particular
> purpose it would not be very hard to do.
> Frederico Fonseca
> ema il: frederico_fonseca at syssoft-int.com


Well, in part we don't have the time required.
In part the original conversion program was supposed to do the job,
and didn't.

It isn't as simple at you might suspect - you have to take all the
COBOL reserved names into consideration (in the version of COBOL you
are using), as well as text strings in the source that use
continuation marks, all the variations of the different COBOL and SQL
statements and a number of other factors.

I've been told that the reason that the conversion program made the
mess that it did, was that it did not consider the problem statement
by statement, but line by line, which must have been easier to code.

- J.B.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Indent Blues - the woe of auto. conversions to COBOL from 4th gen. lang.

- **From:** Frederico Fonseca <real-email-in-msg-spam@email.com>
- **Date:** 2003-09-01T17:07:18+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<lfr6lvgn6fnqf51ptp2cddvs2ds59o2tb4@4ax.com>`
- **References:** `<fed16812.0308290148.50d4a43a@posting.google.com> <a3eukvg38dd9m8f2uht1b6s9ra7pp7294n@4ax.com> <fed16812.0309010724.7cf292cb@posting.google.com>`

```
On 1 Sep 2003 08:24:31 -0700, noname21@iname.com (J.B. Martin) wrote:

>> Why don't you guys write your own program. Just for that particular
>> purpose it would not be very hard to do.
…[18 more quoted lines elided]…
>- J.B.

As I said it should be easy, but I may be wrong.

Could you post a few examples (including one of the bad ones) of how
the indentation is wrong, and of how you would like to have it? I have
already some programs to do this type of conversion and maybe one of
them can be easly adapted to your needs.

You are also refering to lines with "continuation marks". By this I
assume it is the "-" in column 7? Why would these particular lines be
"badly" indented?





Frederico Fonseca
ema il: frederico_fonseca at syssoft-int.com
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Indent Blues - the woe of auto. conversions to COBOL from 4th gen. lang.

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** noname21@iname.com (J.B. Martin)
- **Date:** 2003-09-02T07:16:37-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<fed16812.0309020616.2d6863df@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<fed16812.0308290148.50d4a43a@posting.google.com> <a3eukvg38dd9m8f2uht1b6s9ra7pp7294n@4ax.com> <fed16812.0309010724.7cf292cb@posting.google.com> <lfr6lvgn6fnqf51ptp2cddvs2ds59o2tb4@4ax.com>`

```
Frederico Fonseca <real-email-in-msg-spam@email.com> wrote in message news:<lfr6lvgn6fnqf51ptp2cddvs2ds59o2tb4@4ax.com>...
> 
> As I said it should be easy, but I may be wrong.
…[8 more quoted lines elided]…
> "badly" indented?

In regards to the '-' marks, a program that reindents the code would
have to be carefull that it change the content of the text.

I'll show you a single medium sized example that has been corrected
for all syntax errors.

Note that there are no EXEC SQL - END EXEC commands in this example,
which there often are in the code I'm working on. I've only left one
continuation mark in the code.

A nice re-indent program would simply do the job I've done here, a
good one would give me a few options as to how many spaces I wanted to
indent the code, and a great re-indent program would gather the lines
of code that have been practially broken up into a word per line,
utilizeing the free'd space on the right side of the code.

regards
- J.B.

one thing you can't see from this example, the section following this
one started off indented at the same colum that this one left ended
on!!

CODE is now:

123456 DATECONTROL         SECTION.
123456     PERFORM OPEN-FIRST-MAGN10-02
123456     EVALUATE TRUE
123456     WHEN OK-SW1
123456         PERFORM OPEN-FIRST-MAGN11-01
123456         EVALUATE TRUE
123456         WHEN OK-SW1
123456             IF  MMAA110 IN LLFFMAGN10 NOT = MMAA110 IN
LLFFMAGN11
123456                 AND MMAA148 IN LLFFMAGN11 = 'J'
123456                 IF  ZPDW012 IN LLFFMAGN10 = 'A' AND
123456                     MMAA148 IN LLFFMAGN10 = 'N'
123456                     CONTINUE
123456                 ELSE
123456                     MOVE SPACE TO TTEXT IN ERROR-PAR
123456                     STRING
123456                           'BY LATER TASK IN AT '
123456                           'AND START OVER '
123456                            DELIMITED BY SIZE INTO
123456                            TTEXT IN ERROR-PAR
123456                     MOVE 'PSASDF07' TO TTEXTNR IN ERROR-PAR
123456                 END-IF
123456             END-IF
123456         WHEN NOT-FOUN
123456           CONTINUE
123456         END-EVALUATE
123456         PERFORM CLOSE-FIRST-MAGN11-01
123456         IF  ZPDW012 IN LLFFMAGN10 = 'A'
123456             PERFORM UNTIL SQL-EOF
123456                 PERFORM FETCH-EACH-AA9010-01
123456                 EVALUATE TRUE
123456                 WHEN OK-SW1
123456                 WHEN NOT-FOUN
123456                     MOVE 'NOTFO' TO FEJL IN ERROR-PAR
123456                 END-EVALUATE
123456             END-PERFORM
123456             PERFORM CLOSE-EACH-AA9010-01
123456         ELSE
123456             MOVE 'ORKEN' TO CHECK-ALT IN WRK1
123456         END-IF
123456         MOVE 'ORKEN' TO DATO-SAT IN WRK1
123456     WHEN NOT-FOUN
123456         GO TO X-DATECONTROL
123456     END-EVALUATE
123456     PERFORM UNTIL SQL-EOF
123456         PERFORM FETCH-EACH-AA9010-02
123456         EVALUATE TRUE
123456         WHEN OK-SW1
123456             PERFORM CHK-RISIKO-TXT
123456             EVALUATE FGR0201 IN LLFFAA9010
123456             WHEN 101
123456             WHEN 103
123456                 PERFORM CHECK-10X
123456             WHEN 401
123456             WHEN 402
123456                 PERFORM CHECK-401
123456             END-EVALUATE
123456             IF  ZPDW007 IN LLFFAA9010 NOT = 999999
123456                 IF  ZPDW012 IN LLFFAA9010 = 'A'
123456                     IF  ZPDW009 IN LLFFAA9010 = TYPEDATE IN
WRK1
123456                         CONTINUE
123456                     ELSE
123456                         MOVE SPACE  TO TTEXT IN ERROR-PAR
123456                         STRING
123456                            'DATE OF RISK ISNT '
123456                            ' IN COMPLIANCE WITH THE POLICIES  '
123456                           DELIMITED BY SIZE INTO
123456                                            TTEXT IN ERROR-PAR
123456                     END-IF
123456                 ELSE
123456                     IF  ZPDW010 IN LLFFAA9010 = 'N'
123456                         MOVE TYPEDATE IN WRK1
123456                              TO TYPEDATE IN KRIT-DE0010-02
123456                         PERFORM OPEN-FIRST-DE0010-02
123456                             MOVE 'N' TO SQL-DELSW
123456                             PERFORM FETCH-FIRST-DE0010-02
123456                                 EVALUATE TRUE
123456                                 WHEN OK-SW1
123456                                 WHEN NOT-FOUN
123456                                     MOVE SPACE
123456                                          TO TTEXT IN ERROR-PAR
123456                                     STRING
123456                                         'OPRET MINDST EN '
123456                                            FAA9102 IN
LLFFAA9010
123456                                            DELIMITED BY SIZE
INTO
123456                                            TTEXT IN ERROR-PAR
123456                                     MOVE 'PS031009'
123456                                          TO TTEXTNR IN
ERROR-PAR
123456                                     PERFORM DB2-GOBACK
123456                                 END-EVALUATE
123456                                 PERFORM CLOSE-FIRST-DE0010-02
123456                                 END-IF
123456                             END-IF
123456                         END-IF
123456                     WHEN NOT-FOUN
123456                        MOVE SPACE TO TTEXT IN ERROR-PAR
123456                        STRING
123456                               'DER EKSISTERER INGEN RISICI '
123456                               'SOM SAG Pï¿½ RISIKON'
123456                               DELIMITED BY SIZE INTO
123456                               TTEXT IN ERROR-PAR
123456                         MOVE 'PS0SFS11' TO TTEXTNR IN ERROR-PAR
123456                         PERFORM DB2-GOBACK
123456                     END-EVALUATE
123456                 END-PERFORM
123456                 PERFORM CLOSE-EACH-AA9010-02
123456                 MOVE POLNR IN IPS0310I TO POLNR IN
KRIT-DE0010-03
123456                 PERFORM OPEN-EACH-DE0010-03
123456                     MOVE 'N' TO SQL-DELSW
123456                     PERFORM UNTIL SQL-EOF
123456                         PERFORM FETCH-EACH-DE0010-03
123456                             EVALUATE TRUE
123456                             WHEN OK-SW1
123456                                 IF  ZPDW007
123456                                    IN LLFFDE0010 NOT = 999999
123456                                     EVALUATE ZPDW012
123456                                              IN LLFFDE0010
123456                                     WHEN 'A'
123456                                         IF  ZPDW009
123456                                 IN LLFFDE0010 = TYPEDATE IN
WRK1
123456                                             CONTINUE
123456                                         ELSE
123456                         MOVE FDE0101 IN LLFFDE0010 TO PIC-NUM8
123456                         MOVE SPACE  TO TTEXT IN ERROR-PAR
123456                         STRING

123456                           ' STEMMER IKKE OVERENS MED POLICIES
',
123456                                             PIC-NUM8
123456                                            DELIMITED BY SIZE
INTO

123456                                          TO TTEXTNR IN
ERROR-PAR

123456                                         END-IF
123456                                     WHEN OTHER
123456                                         EVALUATE ZPDW010
123456                                                  IN LLFFMAGN10
123456                                         WHEN 'N'
123456                                             IF  ZPDW008
123456                                IN LLFFDE0010 >= TYPEDATE IN
WRK1

123456                                 IN LLFFDE0010 = TYPEDATE IN
WRK1
123456                                             CONTINUE

ERROR-PAR

CODE should be:

123456 DATECONTROL         SECTION.
123456     PERFORM OPEN-FIRST-MAGN10-02
123456     EVALUATE TRUE
123456     WHEN OK-SW1
123456       PERFORM OPEN-FIRST-MAGN11-01
123456       EVALUATE TRUE
123456       WHEN OK-SW1
123456         IF  MMAA110 IN LLFFMAGN10 NOT = MMAA110 IN LLFFMAGN11
123456             AND MMAA148 IN LLFFMAGN11 = 'J'
123456           IF  ZPDW012 IN LLFFMAGN10 = 'A' AND
123456              MMAA148 IN LLFFMAGN10 = 'N'
123456             CONTINUE
123456           ELSE
123456             MOVE SPACE TO TTEXT IN ERROR-PAR

123456               'BY LATER TASK IN AT '
123456               'AND START OVER '
123456               DELIMITED BY SIZE INTO
123456               TTEXT IN ERROR-PAR

123456           END-IF
123456         END-IF
123456       WHEN NOT-FOUN

123456       END-EVALUATE
123456       PERFORM CLOSE-FIRST-MAGN11-01
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Indent Blues - the woe of auto. conversions to COBOL from 4th gen. lang.

_(thread depth: 5)_

- **From:** Frederico Fonseca <real-email-in-msg-spam@email.com>
- **Date:** 2003-09-02T15:50:45+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3ib9lvsecfvp8h1ibhd02q8pkn1ub87j61@4ax.com>`
- **References:** `<fed16812.0308290148.50d4a43a@posting.google.com> <a3eukvg38dd9m8f2uht1b6s9ra7pp7294n@4ax.com> <fed16812.0309010724.7cf292cb@posting.google.com> <lfr6lvgn6fnqf51ptp2cddvs2ds59o2tb4@4ax.com> <fed16812.0309020616.2d6863df@posting.google.com>`

```
On 2 Sep 2003 07:16:37 -0700, noname21@iname.com (J.B. Martin) wrote:

>Frederico Fonseca <real-email-in-msg-spam@email.com> wrote in message news:<lfr6lvgn6fnqf51ptp2cddvs2ds59o2tb4@4ax.com>...
>> 
…[32 more quoted lines elided]…
>on!!
Geez. I see your problem. It is worst than what I imagined.
I am just very surprised that the conversion program did not do a
correct alignment between start/end of groups (e.g. I would expect
every "end-perform" to be aligned with the corresponding "perform").

I would be able to solve only the changing of the indenting from 4 to
2 positions, but more than that would involve a lot of work.

(The continuation lines are "easy" to deal with (read ahead before
processing the line). I personally would NEVER use them on the
procedure division though).

Sorry for not being able to help more.




Frederico Fonseca
ema il: frederico_fonseca at syssoft-int.com
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Indent Blues - the woe of auto. conversions to COBOL from 4th gen. lang.

_(thread depth: 5)_

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2003-09-02T20:24:19+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<T%65b.22656$Om1.22521@newsread2.news.atl.earthlink.net>`
- **References:** `<fed16812.0308290148.50d4a43a@posting.google.com> <a3eukvg38dd9m8f2uht1b6s9ra7pp7294n@4ax.com> <fed16812.0309010724.7cf292cb@posting.google.com> <lfr6lvgn6fnqf51ptp2cddvs2ds59o2tb4@4ax.com> <fed16812.0309020616.2d6863df@posting.google.com>`

```
I don't know what the pre-conversion code looked like, but it seems to me
(from this example) that what you REALLY need to do (which would be  QUITE
difficult with an automated system) would be to take some of the "nested"
logic and move it into separately performed routines (paragraphs /
sections).  As I RECALL, this was an IBM mainframe "problem". If so, what I
would do (and this MIGHT be possible to do via an automated system) would be
to compile the code and look at (manually or via an automated system) the
"nesting level" information in the listing.  Then I would take all the lines
from (for example) nesting level 4 and above and "move" them to a new
paragraph and perform that from the original source code location.  Then in
the "new" paragraph, you could start at column 12 and indent
"intelligently".

This sounds (to me) to be a pretty resource intensive project.  I guess that
(assuming the code from the conversion process actually works "as required")
that this NOT be done as a project by itself, but that it be done during
"normal" maintenance of each program.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Indent Blues - the woe of auto. conversions to COBOL from 4th gen. lang.

_(thread depth: 5)_

- **From:** "Harley" <dennis.harleyNoSpam@worldnet.att.net>
- **Date:** 2003-09-03T11:53:49+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<hDk5b.124079$0v4.9015562@bgtnsc04-news.ops.worldnet.att.net>`
- **References:** `<fed16812.0308290148.50d4a43a@posting.google.com> <a3eukvg38dd9m8f2uht1b6s9ra7pp7294n@4ax.com> <fed16812.0309010724.7cf292cb@posting.google.com> <lfr6lvgn6fnqf51ptp2cddvs2ds59o2tb4@4ax.com> <fed16812.0309020616.2d6863df@posting.google.com>`

```

"J.B. Martin" <noname21@iname.com> wrote in message
news:fed16812.0309020616.2d6863df@posting.google.com...
<<snip>>

WOW.

There are 2 things you can do:

1. There are COBOL restructuring tools available, you should be able to see
one from IBM listed in you ISPF menus. (Sorry, don't remember which panel.)
You may have to get the product installed.
The restructuring tool will clean up the nesting problem.

2. You need to change the program data names.
You can create an ISPF macro (CLIST, or REXX) to change the names to
something a bit more readable.

Change the names before you restructure.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Indent Blues - the woe of auto. conversions to COBOL from 4th gen. lang.

_(thread depth: 5)_

- **From:** "Ira Baxter" <idbaxter@semdesigns.com>
- **Date:** 2003-09-03T14:57:01-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3f564615$1@giga.realtime.net>`
- **References:** `<fed16812.0308290148.50d4a43a@posting.google.com> <a3eukvg38dd9m8f2uht1b6s9ra7pp7294n@4ax.com> <fed16812.0309010724.7cf292cb@posting.google.com> <lfr6lvgn6fnqf51ptp2cddvs2ds59o2tb4@4ax.com> <fed16812.0309020616.2d6863df@posting.google.com>`

```
Automatically reindented version of code included.

> I'll show you a single medium sized example that has been corrected
> for all syntax errors.
…[22 more quoted lines elided]…
> 123456     EVALUATE TRUE

I ran this through our COBOL prettyprinter.  Here's the result.
(You need to look at this in an editor that shows
the plain text with a fixed-size font; my newsreader
uses some stupid variable width font even for plain texts,
so the spacing looks uglier here than it really is).

There are a number of options to control various layout issues.
I just took the default setttings, which includes indentation =2
 but you could specify 1 if you want more density to the left.
Your taste may vary, but this took me all of 2 minutes,
much of it spent gluing enough stuff (I just grabbed a header
from another code) on the code
fragment to make it legal again (Our prettyprinters
work by parsing the code completely, so you
have to have a syntactically legal program to get it prettyprinted).

You can learn more about our prettyprinters at the website.
```

```
> J.B. Martin <noname21@iname.com> wrote in message

…[9 more quoted lines elided]…
> Doe this 4GL create COBOL code listings, like TELON does?

No it doesn't. We've had to use some in house conversion program from
a firm in Norway.  It might have been good enough for the job they
needed done, but not our conversion.  We've had to add pre and after
conversion programs to get our job done.

The language we are converting from is IDEAL from CA

- J.B.
```
